title: Probability Theory for Quant
description: Measure theory foundations, probability spaces, random variables, distributions, expectation, conditional probability, and the law of large numbers.

title: Stochastic Calculus
description: Brownian motion, Ito calculus, stochastic differential equations, Ito's lemma, Girsanov theorem, and martingale representation.

title: Linear Algebra for Quantitative Finance
description: Matrix decompositions, eigenvalues, PCA for risk factors, covariance matrices, Cholesky decomposition, and numerical linear algebra.

title: Statistics & Econometrics
description: Regression analysis, hypothesis testing, maximum likelihood estimation, GMM, time series econometrics, and panel data methods.

title: Time Series Analysis
description: ARIMA, GARCH, cointegration, autocorrelation, stationarity tests, VAR models, and spectral analysis for financial data.

title: Derivatives Pricing
description: Black-Scholes model, Greeks, binomial trees, Monte Carlo simulation, exotic options, interest rate derivatives, and volatility surfaces.

title: Portfolio Optimization
description: Mean-variance optimization, efficient frontier, CAPM, factor models, Black-Litterman, risk parity, and robust optimization.

title: Risk Management
description: Value at Risk, Expected Shortfall, stress testing, credit risk models, counterparty risk, Basel regulatory framework, and risk attribution.

title: Algorithmic Trading & Execution
description: Market microstructure, order book dynamics, execution algorithms (VWAP, TWAP), transaction cost analysis, high-frequency strategies, and latency optimization.

title: Machine Learning for Finance
description: Supervised learning for alpha generation, feature engineering for financial data, cross-validation for time series, ensemble methods, and neural networks for price prediction.

title: Fixed Income Analytics
description: Bond pricing, yield curves, duration and convexity, term structure models, mortgage-backed securities, and credit spread modeling.

title: Numerical Methods in Finance
description: Finite difference methods, Monte Carlo simulation, quasi-random sequences, numerical PDE solvers, calibration algorithms, and root-finding methods.

title: Python for Quantitative Finance
description: NumPy, Pandas, SciPy for financial computation, vectorized operations, data pipelines, API integration with market data providers, and backtesting frameworks.

title: Volatility Modeling
description: Implied vs realized volatility, GARCH family models, stochastic volatility (Heston, SABR), volatility surfaces, variance swaps, and VIX.

title: Quant Interview & Problem Solving
description: Brain teasers, probability puzzles, mental math, coding challenges, options pricing on the spot, and market-making scenarios.
